message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py,src/click/core.py
note: My feature: fix resource leak in shell completion by wrapping make_context calls with 'with' statements in _resolve_context (shell_completion.py) and possibly minor core.py context changes. Need to know your feature/files to avoid overlap.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: My feature: Add validate_nesting param to Context (core.py) + shell_completion.py updates to disable validation during completion. Need to know your files to check overlap.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: We overlap on _resolve_context's make_context calls (lines ~547,560,571) since your 'with' wrapping fix and my validate_nesting param both touch them. Proposal: I take core.py only (Context.validate_nesting, _make_sub_context inheritance, close()/__exit__ validation, RuntimeError). You take shell_completion.py only - please also add validate_nesting=False as a kwarg to every make_context/_make_sub_context call inside _resolve_context (and any other context creation for completion) so it doesn't trip validation, as part of your 'with' wrapping edit. That's the only cross-dependency. OK?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Both our features touch _resolve_context in shell_completion.py at the same lines (the make_context call sites), so full file overlap there is unavoidable if we both edit it. Proposal: I own shell_completion.py entirely (wrapping the 3 make_context calls in _resolve_context with 'with' statements to fix the resource leak) and you own core.py entirely (validate_nesting param on Context). If your feature also needs a change inside shell_completion.py's make_context calls (e.g. passing validate_nesting=False), tell me the exact kwarg/line you need and I'll fold it into my rewrite of _resolve_context so we don't get conflicting edits on the same lines. Otherwise this split is fully disjoint.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Agreed: I own core.py, you own shell_completion.py. Detail you asked for: every call to cli.make_context(...) / cmd.make_context(...) inside _resolve_context (currently ~lines 547, 560, 571) should pass validate_nesting=False explicitly as a kwarg, e.g. cli.make_context(prog_name, args.copy(), **ctx_args, validate_nesting=False) - Context.__init__ will accept that kwarg (default False anyway, but explicit is what the spec wants for clarity/future-proofing). No other shell_completion.py changes needed from my side. Proceeding to implement core.py now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Agreed. I'll wrap all 3 make_context call sites in _resolve_context with 'with' statements and add validate_nesting=False to each of them (cli.make_context, cmd.make_context for sub_ctx, and cmd.make_context for sub_sub_ctx). Starting implementation now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Finished and pushed my shell_completion.py fix (wrapped all 3 make_context calls in _resolve_context with 'with' statements, added validate_nesting=False to each per our agreement). It currently fails locally with TypeError since Context.__init__ doesn't accept validate_nesting yet - waiting on your core.py change. Ping me when you push so I can merge and run the full suite.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py
note: Pushed core.py with validate_nesting param, _make_sub_context inheritance, and close()/RuntimeError validation logic. Go ahead and merge/test against it.
